Jae Woong Hwang
Resume Experience Toolchains

Selected Toolchain Project

ADAS Log-to-AI Workbench

MF4 preprocessing, model training, normalization, and ONNX/MATLAB export workflow for ADAS engineers.

Python MF4 / ASAM MDF4 PyTorch ONNX MATLAB Export
01

Context

The ADAS team collected real-vehicle and calibration data mainly through CANape in MF4 format. Each module owner needed a repeatable way to turn raw measurement logs into model-ready datasets for AI-based experiments.

The workbench was designed around the existing engineering workflow: load the log, select useful signals, trim the usable range, normalize the data, run a module-specific model script, and export the trained result for later validation.

02

Problem

Without a shared workflow, each developer had to repeatedly write custom scripts for trimming, signal selection, normalization, training, evaluation, and export. That made AI experimentation slow, inconsistent, and difficult to compare across modules.

The bottleneck was not only model training. The bigger issue was converting real ADAS measurement logs into a clean, reproducible AI workflow that module owners could reuse.
03

Constraint

The tool had to fit engineers who were not trying to build a full ML platform from scratch. It also had to stay compatible with existing MF4 measurement files, Python-based model scripts, normalization settings, and downstream simulation or bypass workflows.

  • Input data came from real calibration/measurement logs rather than curated ML datasets.
  • Different module owners could have different model architectures and hyperparameters.
  • The export path had to support portable model artifacts, not only local Python execution.
04

Decision

I designed a GUI-based workbench where engineers can load MF4 data, preprocess selected signals, configure model training, run independently written Python training scripts, and export trained artifacts to ONNX or MATLAB-compatible formats.

The key design choice was to separate the shared data workflow from the model-owner-specific training logic. This keeps preprocessing standardized while still allowing each developer to plug in their own model implementation.

05

Impact

The tool gives each module owner a reusable path from vehicle logs to AI model artifacts. It reduces duplicated preprocessing work and makes experiments easier to standardize across ADAS functions.

For module ownersLess time rewriting conversion scripts; more time evaluating model behavior.
For validation workflowA cleaner path from log-based training to ONNX/MATLAB-oriented artifacts.
06

Implementation

  • MF4/CANape log loading workflow for ADAS time-series data.
  • Signal selection and preprocessing pipeline for trimming, alignment, and normalization.
  • Dataset export path for preprocessed signals.
  • Configurable training workflow with hyperparameter settings.
  • Support for independently written model scripts by individual module owners.
  • Result visualization and evaluation page for model output review.
  • ONNX and MATLAB-oriented export path for simulation or controlled validation workflows.
07

Public-Safe Boundary

This page contains a public-safe workflow summary only. Proprietary vehicle logs, internal signal names, model parameters, calibration values, datasets, and company-specific validation criteria are intentionally omitted.
